retrieved.title: Studying safely
retrieved.text:
The University is a large community. Students, academics and admin-istrative staff meet on site and digitally. The following website provides tips and information and the most important contact points for working and studying successfully together.

The emergency text message service notifies you directly via text message in case of a sudden emergency (such as a major fire) at a location of the University of Vienna. It thus helps ensure safe university operations.

Students can register for the emergency text message service on a voluntary basis with their private phone number on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms). You can deregister from the service at any time.

Further information such as about data protection is available on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms) and in the [Data Protection Declaration of the University of Vienna](https://dsba.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/p_dsba/datenschutzerklaerung_websites_V04_26062020_EN.pdf).

[### Register now!](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

The buildings of the University of Vienna are subject to rules, such as the House Rules or the Laboratory and Workshop Regulations in laboratories. For your own and other people’s safety, information about the procedure in case of fire or accidents is available.

© University of Vienna / Gebhart Sengmüller

- [Security management](https://rrm.univie.ac.at/en/services/security/)
- [Threat management – non-violent university](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/violence-free-university-prevention-and-support)
- [House Rules](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/house-rules/)
- [Laboratory and Workshop Regulations](https://intra.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/upload/rrm/ANS/Downloads/Laborordnung/LABOR-und_WERKSTA%CC%88TTENORDNUNG_EN.pdf)

- [General Fire Safety Regulations](https://rrm.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/d_rrm/Downloads/2015_2016_Brandschutzordnung.pdf) (in German)
- [Procedure in case of student accidents](#c1215109)
- [Student’s Union (ÖH) insurance](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/services/insurance-policy-austrian-students-union-oh)
- [Emergency text message](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

retrieved.title: Studying safely
heading_path: Procedure in case of student accidents
retrieved.text: What should I do if a fellow student or I have an accident while studying? Accidents have to be reported. Please note that accident reports concerning students in connection with their studies must be submitted electronically. We ask you to please NO longer use any previous PDF forms or copy templates that you may have saved. The most recent version of the form “Accident report for persons in educational institutions” can be used by students who have had an accident at the university or on the way to or from the university. It is completed via the AUVA website ([auva.at/unfallmeldung](http://auva.at/unfallmeldung)). 1. Students fill out the form and select the option “Forward at the educational institution”. 2. Students enter the name and email address of the director of studies. If the accident occurred in a service facility, e.g. in the university library, USI, etc., then the email goes to the DLE management there. In cases of doubt, the report can also be sent to the management of the DLE Study Services and Teaching ([slw.leitung@univie.ac.at](mailto:slw.leitung@univie.ac.at)). 3. The head of the department or the head of the division will be notified and will then forward this form electronically to the AUVA. 4. If you have any questions about the accident report, the AUVA service team will be happy to help at any time. Contact the AUVA at: E-Mail: [wla@auva.at](mailto:wla@auva.at), Tel.: +43 5 9393

retrieved.title: Education Fairs
retrieved.text:
[## You've got further questions?](/en/contact-form/)

[Choosing the topic "degree programme and admission in general" or "Schule trifft Uni" in the contact form is the best way to reach the responsible office.](/en/contact-form/)

retrieved.title: The admission procedure at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
Please use our [contact form](/en/contact-form/). It allows you to be more flexible and to have your request documented in written form.

For reasons of data protection, we cannot provide any information related to personal data on the phone.

You can reach us via telephone by calling +43/1/4277-12100 at the following times:

- Monday and Thursday: from 12:30 p.m. to 01:30 p.m.

Please take note of the working hours of the Admission Office for the timely submission of applications to the Admission Office that are not submitted via u:space. You can find a summary of the working hours [in this proclamation](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Zulassung/Kundmachung_zur_rechtswirksamen_Einbringung_von_Anbringen.pdf) (in German only).

[## Appointment online first-time admission](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/)

“Drawing a number and waiting in line” is old news. Comfortably complete your first-time admission at home via video chat. Here you can find detailed [info regarding booking your appointment for online first-time admission.](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/)

© Universität Wien/derknopfdruecker.com

[## Online info-presentations](/en/admission/online-info-presentations/)

You are interested in studying at the University of Vienna? Come to our info-presentations! In the presentations you will learn everything you need to know for your application to admission to a degree programme. During and after the presentation you can ask your questions in the chat.

It does not matter if you are already in Vienna, or listening from another place: you can comfortably attend the presentation via your own device. Here you can find details regarding [online info-presentation](/en/admission/online-info-presentations/).

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: / - Inskription obsolete - see [Admission](/en/admission-procedure/) - Instructions on the right to appeal Official notifications issued by the University of Vienna are provided with instructions on the right to appeal. The instructions on the right to appeal at the end of an official notification include information on the possibilty, form and deadline for lodging an appeal against an official notification. Related entries: - [Application for admission to a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30197&cHash=d1463d320886c313223dcda9a42bda02) - Insurance A part of the Students’ Union fee you pay covers your casualty and personal liability protection within the buildings/university grounds of the University of Vienna. The insurance enters into force as soon as the Students’ Union fee is registered as paid. This insurance is no health insurance. Students must apply for health insurance at the health insurance fund of their choice. Related Links: - [Austrian National Union of Students - insurance](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/service/insurance) Related entries: - [Austrian National Union of Students (ÖH)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31211&cHash=cc183cd54b32092671cf06b22d1ad46f) - International Office The International Office informs students and employees of the University of Vienna about mobility programmes and enables them to participate in them. Together with faculties, the International Office works on the development of internationalization programs for students, academics and general university staff. Related Links: - [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/) Related entries: - [Semester abroad](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30817&cHash=d6ad15336e091fb32b403f20ecc4ae06) - [Erasmus+](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30864&cHash=ea57c8821cd2999c69dcc17641651743) - [Mobility programmes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31188&cHash=813707a12daceba2774512659b917d60) - Introductory and Orientation Period (STEOP) The Introductory and Orientation Period (STEOP) is a chance for students

retrieved.title: Confirmations for (former) students
retrieved.text:
The Admission Office may also confirm information about the admission to degree programmes in forms provided by other authorities (e.g. in the form for orphan's allowance). Information that cannot be confirmed will be crossed out in the form or explained by added comments. This is, for example, the prospective date of completion of the degree programme.

Fill in the form with your personal data and send all pages of the form combined into one PDF file via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Always send all pages of the form – even if other pages contain information that cannot be confirmed by the Admission Office. We will send you the completed, stamped and signed form as PDF file. If you need the confirmation via post please let us know in your message. Make sure that your current address is stored in u:space at Personal matters > Personal data.

Former students who do not have an active u:account (anymore) can send their request including all attachments via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

Please note: BAföG forms can only be confirmed by the [StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

Military or civil service is not a special case of hardship according to § 26 para 3 military law. You can download your confirmation of enrolment in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time.

During your military or civil service you can be on [leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/).

retrieved.title: Confirmations for third parties
retrieved.text:
Confirmations for external institutions, parents and other third parties may only be issued if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and copy of the personal ID of the person concerned. Exceptions apply for lawyers who request information about a person represented by them as well as in the course of the obligatory exchange of information between local or government authorities ("Amtshilfe").

Please always directly contact the person concerned in the first place due to data protection regulations. Current students can download their student documents directly in u:space > Personal matters > My documents or request special confirmations via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Former students message us at [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

If you are unable to receive the confirmation by the person concerned you can contact [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Describe which information has to be confirmed in your message. In any case include the name, date of birth and (if known) student ID number of the person.

For information regarding examination results please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter of the respective degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

The Admission Office may issue confirmations for third parties if they provide a signed authorisation (power of attorney) of the person concerned. Send the authorisation, a scan of the valid personal ID of the person concerned and a scan of your own personal ID via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need in your message.

retrieved.title: Confirmations
retrieved.text: request/confirmation is available as bilingual document (German and English). - Confirmation of withdrawal If you close one (or multiple) of your degree programmes you can download a confirmation of withdrawal for this programme. For successfully completed programmes a confirmation of withdrawal cannot be issued. The confirmation of withdrawal is available in German and English.

retrieved.title: Studying safely
retrieved.text:
Do not leave your personal belongings unattended in public areas of the University of Vienna.

This also applies to Student Spaces and course rooms.
In the event of theft, you can report it to any police station.

Students spend a lot of time in the digital realm, for example, to participate in courses, to research literature at the library or to interact with fellow students. The following information aims to contribute to your safety online.

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

- [IT security: Tips for users](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/services/it-security/)
- [12 IT security commandments for users](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/it-news/article/news/12-it-security-gebote-fuer-nutzerinnen/?no_cache=1)
- [Password management](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/password/)
- [Tips regarding the password](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/password-tips/)

- [Social media and blog netiquette](https://blog.univie.ac.at/en/netiquette/)
- [How to prevent Zoombombing](https://zid.univie.ac.at/it-news/artikel/news/keine-angst-vor-zoombombing/) (in German)

Participating, asking questions, considering issues from different perspectives and interacting with others are central aspects of studying, but can also lead to conflict. You can contact the following organisational units to seek help for yourself or for all conflict parties, or to establish a network that can help you in case of problems.

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

- [ÖH Helpline](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/helpline/)
- [Psychological Counselling Service](https://www.studierendenberatung.at/en/)
- [Accessible Studying Team of the University of Vienna](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/accessible-studies/counselling/)
- [Sexual Harassment and Bullying Counselling Office](https://personalwesen.univie.ac.at/en/gender-equality-diversity/sexual-harassment-bullying-office/)
- [AKGleich - Equal Opportunities Working Party](https://gleichbehandlung.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Arbiration Commission of the University of Vienna](https://schiedskommission.univie.ac.at/en/)

retrieved.title: Confirmations for (former) students
retrieved.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents themselves at any time. The documents carry an official electronic signature and are accepted by official authorities.

On this page you can find an overview of the most common confirmations and information on where to receive them.

The student documents (student record sheet, confirmation of enrolment and confirmation of study periods) confirm your admission at the University of Vienna. Always try to hand in these officially electronically signed documents at the authority requesting the confirmation first. You can download your study documents in u:space.

You need a confirmation that contains information that is not contained in the documents available in u:space? Or confirmations with "wet" stamp and signature? Please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Answer the following questions in your request:

- What should be confirmed?
- For which authority shall the confirmation be issued?
- Do you need a "wet" stamp and signature or official electronic signature?
- Shall the confirmation be issued in English or German?

retrieved.title: Information for asylum seekers
retrieved.text:
If you have questions regarding admission at the University of Vienna: [Admission Office](/en/admission-procedure/#c510461)

If you have questions regarding your study programme: [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

If you have questions regarding having a previous degree recognised: [Office of the Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/nostrification/)

The Austrian Students’ Union (ÖH) offers extensive counselling: [Office for Antiracism Work (and International/Foreign Students)](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/offices/office-for-antiracism-work)

Due to the data protection regulation, we cannot provide any information related to personal data to third parties.

retrieved.title: Studying safely
retrieved.text:
No smoking.

The only thing that is smoking here are our brains.

It is prohibited to handle flames or flammable liquids.

Drinking enough water will keep you fit and alert.

Access restrictions: stay away from the marked hazard zones.

Icon: Use the University’s study spaces and Student Spaces. (student while reading)

Use the University’s study spaces and Student Spaces.

Animals are not allowed inside. Exceptions apply to guide, assistance and other service dogs.

You can bring along your lucky charm.

Be quiet.

Science-based, respectful and appreciative discussions are expressly wanted.

Sports equipment may not be used inside the buildings.

Please train your mind as much as you want here.

It is prohibited to wear, carry or bring weapons of any kind to the University.

A razor-sharp mind is the only weapon allowed.

In addition to the security management team and the porters, you can also contact the [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnits (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/), your [teachers](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) and the employees of the different organisational units in case of incidents.

retrieved.title: Deadlines and dates
retrieved.text:
Any questions about the admission procedure?

Message us via the contact form. You can reach us by phone on Mondays and Thursdays from 12:30 to 13:30.

[+43-1-4277-12100](tel:+43-1-4277-12100)
[To the contact form](/en/contact)

Do you have questions about registering for courses and examinations? Or about grades and the recognition of exams? The StudiesServiceCenters (SSC) and StudiesServiceUnits (SSS) are the right place to go.

[To the SSCs](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/studies/students/organisation-of-studies-and-teaching)

Would you like to know more about support services such as STEOP mentoring or writing workshops? The team at the CTL looks forward to answering your questions.

[Email](#)
[To the CTL website](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en)

retrieved.title: The day of the written test
retrieved.text:
Before the test, you will receive detailed instructions on how to proceed (for example, how to mark your answers or how to correct incorrectly marked answers). The exact end time of the test will also be announced.

Take a look at the sample exercises and possible questions for the tests on our test details website.

[View test details](/en/through-the-entrance-exam/test-details)

©
Universität Wien

Previous

©

Next

For organizational reasons, you may not leave the test hall early. Please take this into account when planning your schedule and travel arrangements. Upon request, you can obtain time confirmations (for military service, civil service, etc.) after the written test.

We will inform you of your test result and your next steps in the admission procedure via e-mail.

©
Universität Wien

Previous

©

Next

Collapse all

Open all

If you need an adaptation of the written test, this must be stated when you apply for admission. You did not do so and need an electronic device for health reasons? Please contact us as soon as possible via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/929). Let us know exactly which devices you need. A diagnosis is not required.

[Information about the entrance exam procedure for applicants with disabilities](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/entrance-exam/entrance-exam-procedures-for-applicants-with-disabilities/)

No, this is not possible. You need to bring your printed permission to attend in the format A4.

retrieved.title: Confirmations
retrieved.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations themselves in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time. For confirmations issued in English you first switch the language u:space is displayed in to English. These confirmations carry an official electronic signature and are accepted at official authorities.

External institutions, parents and third parties only receive confirmations if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and a copy of the personal ID of the person concerned.

retrieved.title: Confirmations for (former) students
retrieved.text:
If you do not study at the University of Vienna anymore send your request to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need. Include a scan of your valid personal ID in the format JPEG or PDF. If your name has changed since your time at the University of Vienna, also add an official proof of the change of name (e.g. marriage certificate) to your message. Include the following information:

- First and last name
- Date of birth
- Student ID number (if known)
- Degree programme studied
- Beginning and end of studies (if known)

The psychological/psychotherapeutic counselling service for students is available via telephone, e-mail and by prior arrangement also via video call or video chat. For relevant information and contact details, please go to the following website: [studierendenberatung.at](https://www.studierendenberatung.at/en/)

reranked.title: Education Fairs
reranked.text:
[## You've got further questions?](/en/contact-form/)

[Choosing the topic "degree programme and admission in general" or "Schule trifft Uni" in the contact form is the best way to reach the responsible office.](/en/contact-form/)

reranked.title: The admission procedure at the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
Please use our [contact form](/en/contact-form/). It allows you to be more flexible and to have your request documented in written form.

For reasons of data protection, we cannot provide any information related to personal data on the phone.

You can reach us via telephone by calling +43/1/4277-12100 at the following times:

- Monday and Thursday: from 12:30 p.m. to 01:30 p.m.

Please take note of the working hours of the Admission Office for the timely submission of applications to the Admission Office that are not submitted via u:space. You can find a summary of the working hours [in this proclamation](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Zulassung/Kundmachung_zur_rechtswirksamen_Einbringung_von_Anbringen.pdf) (in German only).

[## Appointment online first-time admission](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/)

“Drawing a number and waiting in line” is old news. Comfortably complete your first-time admission at home via video chat. Here you can find detailed [info regarding booking your appointment for online first-time admission.](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/)

© Universität Wien/derknopfdruecker.com

[## Online info-presentations](/en/admission/online-info-presentations/)

You are interested in studying at the University of Vienna? Come to our info-presentations! In the presentations you will learn everything you need to know for your application to admission to a degree programme. During and after the presentation you can ask your questions in the chat.

It does not matter if you are already in Vienna, or listening from another place: you can comfortably attend the presentation via your own device. Here you can find details regarding [online info-presentation](/en/admission/online-info-presentations/).

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: / - Inskription obsolete - see [Admission](/en/admission-procedure/) - Instructions on the right to appeal Official notifications issued by the University of Vienna are provided with instructions on the right to appeal. The instructions on the right to appeal at the end of an official notification include information on the possibilty, form and deadline for lodging an appeal against an official notification. Related entries: - [Application for admission to a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30197&cHash=d1463d320886c313223dcda9a42bda02) - Insurance A part of the Students’ Union fee you pay covers your casualty and personal liability protection within the buildings/university grounds of the University of Vienna. The insurance enters into force as soon as the Students’ Union fee is registered as paid. This insurance is no health insurance. Students must apply for health insurance at the health insurance fund of their choice. Related Links: - [Austrian National Union of Students - insurance](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/service/insurance) Related entries: - [Austrian National Union of Students (ÖH)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31211&cHash=cc183cd54b32092671cf06b22d1ad46f) - International Office The International Office informs students and employees of the University of Vienna about mobility programmes and enables them to participate in them. Together with faculties, the International Office works on the development of internationalization programs for students, academics and general university staff. Related Links: - [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/) Related entries: - [Semester abroad](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30817&cHash=d6ad15336e091fb32b403f20ecc4ae06) - [Erasmus+](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30864&cHash=ea57c8821cd2999c69dcc17641651743) - [Mobility programmes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31188&cHash=813707a12daceba2774512659b917d60) - Introductory and Orientation Period (STEOP) The Introductory and Orientation Period (STEOP) is a chance for students

reranked.title: Confirmations for (former) students
reranked.text:
The Admission Office may also confirm information about the admission to degree programmes in forms provided by other authorities (e.g. in the form for orphan's allowance). Information that cannot be confirmed will be crossed out in the form or explained by added comments. This is, for example, the prospective date of completion of the degree programme.

Fill in the form with your personal data and send all pages of the form combined into one PDF file via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Always send all pages of the form – even if other pages contain information that cannot be confirmed by the Admission Office. We will send you the completed, stamped and signed form as PDF file. If you need the confirmation via post please let us know in your message. Make sure that your current address is stored in u:space at Personal matters > Personal data.

Former students who do not have an active u:account (anymore) can send their request including all attachments via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

Please note: BAföG forms can only be confirmed by the [StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

Military or civil service is not a special case of hardship according to § 26 para 3 military law. You can download your confirmation of enrolment in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time.

During your military or civil service you can be on [leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/).

reranked.title: Confirmations for third parties
reranked.text:
Confirmations for external institutions, parents and other third parties may only be issued if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and copy of the personal ID of the person concerned. Exceptions apply for lawyers who request information about a person represented by them as well as in the course of the obligatory exchange of information between local or government authorities ("Amtshilfe").

Please always directly contact the person concerned in the first place due to data protection regulations. Current students can download their student documents directly in u:space > Personal matters > My documents or request special confirmations via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Former students message us at [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

If you are unable to receive the confirmation by the person concerned you can contact [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). Describe which information has to be confirmed in your message. In any case include the name, date of birth and (if known) student ID number of the person.

For information regarding examination results please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter of the respective degree programme](http://ssc.univie.ac.at/en).

The Admission Office may issue confirmations for third parties if they provide a signed authorisation (power of attorney) of the person concerned. Send the authorisation, a scan of the valid personal ID of the person concerned and a scan of your own personal ID via e-mail to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need in your message.

reranked.title: Confirmations
reranked.text: request/confirmation is available as bilingual document (German and English). - Confirmation of withdrawal If you close one (or multiple) of your degree programmes you can download a confirmation of withdrawal for this programme. For successfully completed programmes a confirmation of withdrawal cannot be issued. The confirmation of withdrawal is available in German and English.

reranked.title: Studying safely
reranked.text:
Do not leave your personal belongings unattended in public areas of the University of Vienna.

This also applies to Student Spaces and course rooms.
In the event of theft, you can report it to any police station.

Students spend a lot of time in the digital realm, for example, to participate in courses, to research literature at the library or to interact with fellow students. The following information aims to contribute to your safety online.

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

- [IT security: Tips for users](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/services/it-security/)
- [12 IT security commandments for users](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/it-news/article/news/12-it-security-gebote-fuer-nutzerinnen/?no_cache=1)
- [Password management](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/password/)
- [Tips regarding the password](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/password-tips/)

- [Social media and blog netiquette](https://blog.univie.ac.at/en/netiquette/)
- [How to prevent Zoombombing](https://zid.univie.ac.at/it-news/artikel/news/keine-angst-vor-zoombombing/) (in German)

Participating, asking questions, considering issues from different perspectives and interacting with others are central aspects of studying, but can also lead to conflict. You can contact the following organisational units to seek help for yourself or for all conflict parties, or to establish a network that can help you in case of problems.

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

- [ÖH Helpline](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/helpline/)
- [Psychological Counselling Service](https://www.studierendenberatung.at/en/)
- [Accessible Studying Team of the University of Vienna](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/accessible-studies/counselling/)
- [Sexual Harassment and Bullying Counselling Office](https://personalwesen.univie.ac.at/en/gender-equality-diversity/sexual-harassment-bullying-office/)
- [AKGleich - Equal Opportunities Working Party](https://gleichbehandlung.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Arbiration Commission of the University of Vienna](https://schiedskommission.univie.ac.at/en/)

reranked.title: Confirmations for (former) students
reranked.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents themselves at any time. The documents carry an official electronic signature and are accepted by official authorities.

On this page you can find an overview of the most common confirmations and information on where to receive them.

The student documents (student record sheet, confirmation of enrolment and confirmation of study periods) confirm your admission at the University of Vienna. Always try to hand in these officially electronically signed documents at the authority requesting the confirmation first. You can download your study documents in u:space.

You need a confirmation that contains information that is not contained in the documents available in u:space? Or confirmations with "wet" stamp and signature? Please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/50). Answer the following questions in your request:

- What should be confirmed?
- For which authority shall the confirmation be issued?
- Do you need a "wet" stamp and signature or official electronic signature?
- Shall the confirmation be issued in English or German?

reranked.title: Information for asylum seekers
reranked.text:
If you have questions regarding admission at the University of Vienna: [Admission Office](/en/admission-procedure/#c510461)

If you have questions regarding your study programme: [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

If you have questions regarding having a previous degree recognised: [Office of the Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/nostrification/)

The Austrian Students’ Union (ÖH) offers extensive counselling: [Office for Antiracism Work (and International/Foreign Students)](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/en/offices/office-for-antiracism-work)

Due to the data protection regulation, we cannot provide any information related to personal data to third parties.

reranked.title: Studying safely
reranked.text:
No smoking.

The only thing that is smoking here are our brains.

It is prohibited to handle flames or flammable liquids.

Drinking enough water will keep you fit and alert.

Access restrictions: stay away from the marked hazard zones.

Icon: Use the University’s study spaces and Student Spaces. (student while reading)

Use the University’s study spaces and Student Spaces.

Animals are not allowed inside. Exceptions apply to guide, assistance and other service dogs.

You can bring along your lucky charm.

Be quiet.

Science-based, respectful and appreciative discussions are expressly wanted.

Sports equipment may not be used inside the buildings.

Please train your mind as much as you want here.

It is prohibited to wear, carry or bring weapons of any kind to the University.

A razor-sharp mind is the only weapon allowed.

In addition to the security management team and the porters, you can also contact the [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnits (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/), your [teachers](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) and the employees of the different organisational units in case of incidents.

reranked.title: Deadlines and dates
reranked.text:
Any questions about the admission procedure?

Message us via the contact form. You can reach us by phone on Mondays and Thursdays from 12:30 to 13:30.

[+43-1-4277-12100](tel:+43-1-4277-12100)
[To the contact form](/en/contact)

Do you have questions about registering for courses and examinations? Or about grades and the recognition of exams? The StudiesServiceCenters (SSC) and StudiesServiceUnits (SSS) are the right place to go.

[To the SSCs](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/studies/students/organisation-of-studies-and-teaching)

Would you like to know more about support services such as STEOP mentoring or writing workshops? The team at the CTL looks forward to answering your questions.

[Email](#)
[To the CTL website](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en)

reranked.title: The day of the written test
reranked.text:
Before the test, you will receive detailed instructions on how to proceed (for example, how to mark your answers or how to correct incorrectly marked answers). The exact end time of the test will also be announced.

Take a look at the sample exercises and possible questions for the tests on our test details website.

[View test details](/en/through-the-entrance-exam/test-details)

©
Universität Wien

Previous

©

Next

For organizational reasons, you may not leave the test hall early. Please take this into account when planning your schedule and travel arrangements. Upon request, you can obtain time confirmations (for military service, civil service, etc.) after the written test.

We will inform you of your test result and your next steps in the admission procedure via e-mail.

©
Universität Wien

Previous

©

Next

Collapse all

Open all

If you need an adaptation of the written test, this must be stated when you apply for admission. You did not do so and need an electronic device for health reasons? Please contact us as soon as possible via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/929). Let us know exactly which devices you need. A diagnosis is not required.

[Information about the entrance exam procedure for applicants with disabilities](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/entrance-exam/entrance-exam-procedures-for-applicants-with-disabilities/)

No, this is not possible. You need to bring your printed permission to attend in the format A4.

reranked.title: Confirmations
reranked.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations themselves in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time. For confirmations issued in English you first switch the language u:space is displayed in to English. These confirmations carry an official electronic signature and are accepted at official authorities.

External institutions, parents and third parties only receive confirmations if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and a copy of the personal ID of the person concerned.

reranked.title: Confirmations for (former) students
reranked.text:
If you do not study at the University of Vienna anymore send your request to [bestaetigungen.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,dguvcgvkiwpigp0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');). State which confirmation you need. Include a scan of your valid personal ID in the format JPEG or PDF. If your name has changed since your time at the University of Vienna, also add an official proof of the change of name (e.g. marriage certificate) to your message. Include the following information:

- First and last name
- Date of birth
- Student ID number (if known)
- Degree programme studied
- Beginning and end of studies (if known)

selected.title: Studying safely
selected.text:
The University is a large community. Students, academics and admin-istrative staff meet on site and digitally. The following website provides tips and information and the most important contact points for working and studying successfully together.

The emergency text message service notifies you directly via text message in case of a sudden emergency (such as a major fire) at a location of the University of Vienna. It thus helps ensure safe university operations.

Students can register for the emergency text message service on a voluntary basis with their private phone number on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms). You can deregister from the service at any time.

Further information such as about data protection is available on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms) and in the [Data Protection Declaration of the University of Vienna](https://dsba.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/p_dsba/datenschutzerklaerung_websites_V04_26062020_EN.pdf).

[### Register now!](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

The buildings of the University of Vienna are subject to rules, such as the House Rules or the Laboratory and Workshop Regulations in laboratories. For your own and other people’s safety, information about the procedure in case of fire or accidents is available.

© University of Vienna / Gebhart Sengmüller

- [Security management](https://rrm.univie.ac.at/en/services/security/)
- [Threat management – non-violent university](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/violence-free-university-prevention-and-support)
- [House Rules](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/house-rules/)
- [Laboratory and Workshop Regulations](https://intra.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/upload/rrm/ANS/Downloads/Laborordnung/LABOR-und_WERKSTA%CC%88TTENORDNUNG_EN.pdf)

- [General Fire Safety Regulations](https://rrm.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/d_rrm/Downloads/2015_2016_Brandschutzordnung.pdf) (in German)
- [Procedure in case of student accidents](#c1215109)
- [Student’s Union (ÖH) insurance](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/services/insurance-policy-austrian-students-union-oh)
- [Emergency text message](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

selected.title: Studying safely
heading_path: Procedure in case of student accidents
selected.text: then forward this form electronically to the AUVA. 4. If you have any questions about the accident report, the AUVA service team will be happy to help at any time. Contact the AUVA at: E-Mail: [wla@auva.at](mailto:wla@auva.at), Tel.: +43 5 9393 31383 (for Vienna, Lower Austria, Burgenland).

selected.title: Studying safely
heading_path: Procedure in case of student accidents
selected.text: What should I do if a fellow student or I have an accident while studying? Accidents have to be reported. Please note that accident reports concerning students in connection with their studies must be submitted electronically. We ask you to please NO longer use any previous PDF forms or copy templates that you may have saved. The most recent version of the form “Accident report for persons in educational institutions” can be used by students who have had an accident at the university or on the way to or from the university. It is completed via the AUVA website ([auva.at/unfallmeldung](http://auva.at/unfallmeldung)). 1. Students fill out the form and select the option “Forward at the educational institution”. 2. Students enter the name and email address of the director of studies. If the accident occurred in a service facility, e.g. in the university library, USI, etc., then the email goes to the DLE management there. In cases of doubt, the report can also be sent to the management of the DLE Study Services and Teaching ([slw.leitung@univie.ac.at](mailto:slw.leitung@univie.ac.at)). 3. The head of the department or the head of the division will be notified and will then forward this form electronically to the AUVA. 4. If you have any questions about the accident report, the AUVA service team will be happy to help at any time. Contact the AUVA at: E-Mail: [wla@auva.at](mailto:wla@auva.at), Tel.: +43 5 9393

[1] Studying safely
The University is a large community. Students, academics and admin-istrative staff meet on site and digitally. The following website provides tips and information and the most important contact points for working and studying successfully together.

The emergency text message service notifies you directly via text message in case of a sudden emergency (such as a major fire) at a location of the University of Vienna. It thus helps ensure safe university operations.

Students can register for the emergency text message service on a voluntary basis with their private phone number on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms). You can deregister from the service at any time.

Further information such as about data protection is available on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms) and in the [Data Protection Declaration of the University of Vienna](https://dsba.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/p_dsba/datenschutzerklaerung_websites_V04_26062020_EN.pdf).

[3] Studying safely — Studying safely on-site. > Procedure in case of student accidents
What should I do if a fellow student or I have an accident while studying? Accidents have to be reported. Please note that accident reports concerning students in connection with their studies must be submitted electronically. We ask you to please NO longer use any previous PDF forms or copy templates that you may have saved. The most recent version of the form “Accident report for persons in educational institutions” can be used by students who have had an accident at the university or on the way to or from the university. It is completed via the AUVA website ([auva.at/unfallmeldung](http://auva.at/unfallmeldung)). 1. Students fill out the form and select the option “Forward at the educational institution”. 2. Students enter the name and email address of the director of studies. If the accident occurred in a service facility, e.g. in the university library, USI, etc., then the email goes to the DLE management there. In cases of doubt, the report can also be sent to the management of the DLE Study Services and Teaching ([slw.leitung@univie.ac.at](mailto:slw.leitung@univie.ac.at)). 3. The head of the department or the head of the division will be notified and will then forward this form electronically to the AUVA. 4. If you have any questions about the accident report, the AUVA service team will be happy to help at any time. Contact the AUVA at: E-Mail: [wla@auva.at](mailto:wla@auva.at), Tel.: +43 5 9393

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Studying safely
The University is a large community. Students, academics and admin-istrative staff meet on site and digitally. The following website provides tips and information and the most important contact points for working and studying successfully together.

The emergency text message service notifies you directly via text message in case of a sudden emergency (such as a major fire) at a location of the University of Vienna. It thus helps ensure safe university operations.

Students can register for the emergency text message service on a voluntary basis with their private phone number on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms). You can deregister from the service at any time.

Further information such as about data protection is available on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms) and in the [Data Protection Declaration of the University of Vienna](https://dsba.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/p_dsba/datenschutzerklaerung_websites_V04_26062020_EN.pdf).

[### Register now!](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

The buildings of the University of Vienna are subject to rules, such as the House Rules or the Laboratory and Workshop Regulations in laboratories. For your own and other people’s safety, information about the procedure in case of fire or accidents is available.

© University of Vienna / Gebhart Sengmüller

- [Security management](https://rrm.univie.ac.at/en/services/security/)
- [Threat management – non-violent university](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/violence-free-university-prevention-and-support)
- [House Rules](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/house-rules/)
- [Laboratory and Workshop Regulations](https://intra.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/upload/rrm/ANS/Downloads/Laborordnung/LABOR-und_WERKSTA%CC%88TTENORDNUNG_EN.pdf)

- [General Fire Safety Regulations](https://rrm.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/d_rrm/Downloads/2015_2016_Brandschutzordnung.pdf) (in German)
- [Procedure in case of student accidents](#c1215109)
- [Student’s Union (ÖH) insurance](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/services/insurance-policy-austrian-students-union-oh)
- [Emergency text message](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

[2] Studying safely — Studying safely on-site. > Procedure in case of student accidents
then forward this form electronically to the AUVA. 4. If you have any questions about the accident report, the AUVA service team will be happy to help at any time. Contact the AUVA at: E-Mail: [wla@auva.at](mailto:wla@auva.at), Tel.: +43 5 9393 31383 (for Vienna, Lower Austria, Burgenland).

[3] Studying safely — Studying safely on-site. > Procedure in case of student accidents
What should I do if a fellow student or I have an accident while studying? Accidents have to be reported. Please note that accident reports concerning students in connection with their studies must be submitted electronically. We ask you to please NO longer use any previous PDF forms or copy templates that you may have saved. The most recent version of the form “Accident report for persons in educational institutions” can be used by students who have had an accident at the university or on the way to or from the university. It is completed via the AUVA website ([auva.at/unfallmeldung](http://auva.at/unfallmeldung)). 1. Students fill out the form and select the option “Forward at the educational institution”. 2. Students enter the name and email address of the director of studies. If the accident occurred in a service facility, e.g. in the university library, USI, etc., then the email goes to the DLE management there. In cases of doubt, the report can also be sent to the management of the DLE Study Services and Teaching ([slw.leitung@univie.ac.at](mailto:slw.leitung@univie.ac.at)). 3. The head of the department or the head of the division will be notified and will then forward this form electronically to the AUVA. 4. If you have any questions about the accident report, the AUVA service team will be happy to help at any time. Contact the AUVA at: E-Mail: [wla@auva.at](mailto:wla@auva.at), Tel.: +43 5 9393
